items.final_wording: Supports documenting small pearly papules, central depression, approximate lesion size, pain status, inflammation, redness, swelling, and interval course.

source.title: Clinical Overview of Molluscum Contagiosum
source.jurisdiction: United States; international infectious-dermatology evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Children and adults with molluscum contagiosum, including people who are immunosuppressed.
source.setting: Primary care, pediatrics, sexual health, and dermatology recognition and review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting characteristic papules, central depression, size, inflammation, redness, swelling, pain context, course, lesion burden, and immunosuppression. It does not generate diagnosis, treatment, procedure, sexual-health conclusion, or follow-up.

exact_location.heading: Clinical features
exact_location.locator: official HCP page, Clinical features section

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting small pearly papules, central depression, approximate lesion size, pain status, inflammation, redness, swelling, and interval course.

items.rationale: The exact CDC clinical-features section supports documenting an established or suspected molluscum review, lesion course, symptoms, inflammation, redness, swelling, site and distribution without inferring diagnosis or treatment need.

items.final_wording: Supports recording pediatric or adult context, lesion number and size, immunosuppression, and secondary bacterial infection concern without inferring a complication.
